$description: The one surface role shadcn has no name for: the modal scrim. Every other surface/content/border role was removed on 2026-07-13 — they were pure aliases of the shadcn color contract (surface.card = {color.card}), so they gave us two names for one value and no new information. The role of each shadcn token now lives in its own $description in semantic/color.json, quoted verbatim from https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/theming ('What it controls'). Hand-owned (NOT regenerated by tokens:migrate).

overlay.$description: Modal scrim (alert-dialog/drawer/sheet): black @ 10% paired with supports-backdrop-filter:backdrop-blur-xs. DESIGN DECISION 2026-07-08: keep this (the rendered behavior); the Figma kit's 30% `background-color` extra (color.background-color) is superseded for scrim use — it remains only as a legacy kit value. Dialog itself renders no scrim. CONSUMED at runtime: bg-(--surface-overlay) in drawer.tsx, alert-dialog.tsx, sheet.tsx.
